Explaining his administration Of
the United tSates reclamation serv-
ice. and virtually defending him
self on the charge made by Sec
rotary Ballinger that he is incom-
petent to handle the work, F. H..
Newell, director of the reclamation
service yesterday spoke to a
sympathetic audience. which inter-
rupted his report of his work with
applause time and time again.


Newell explained that the rush
of settlers for the irrigated lands
was so great that there were many
more applications for land than
there was land available. The gov
eminent is not slow, he declared,
but the settlers impatient to get
water faster than any skill can put
1t on the land. He outlined the
work that has been done in the
last seven years and denied that
there is competition between the
government and private capital.
